title: Full list of Panels 2026 - International Conference on Social Media & Society
snippet: Announcement and schedule for the #SMSociety 2026 conference in Glasgow, UK, listing Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist for the session titled 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable – Where to for Social Media Research?'
description: An announcement for the International Conference on Social Media & Society listing Professor Fabio Giglietto as an expert panelist discussing future pathways for social media research following the closure of CrowdTangle.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino

title: Social media, l'Agcom avvia verifiche con la Commissione Ue sugli algoritmi di Meta. Focus sui contenuti politici
snippet: Milano Finanza reports on Italy's communications regulatory authority (Agcom) initiating a formal consultation with the European Commission. The move was prompted directly by the University of Urbino study led by Fabio Giglietto, investigating the asymmetric effects of Meta's algorithms on democratic discourse.
description: An article reporting on Italy's regulatory authority Agcom initiating a formal consultation with the European Commission, prompted directly by a University of Urbino study led by Fabio Giglietto.

title: Report: "La fermata del Garante" (April 12, 2026 Episode)
snippet: Media coverage and official episode release notes for the investigative television program 'Report' on Rai 3. The segment prominently featured the research findings of Professor Fabio Giglietto, using his data to analyze how tech giants like Meta manage sensitive user data and influence democratic processes through algorithmic visibility curation.
description: An official Rai press release for the investigative program 'Report', highlighting an episode that features Professor Fabio Giglietto's research on Meta's data management and algorithmic visibility curation.

title: Facebook, troppa visibilità a estremisti e complottisti: ecco come impatta Meta sulle elezioni
snippet: An article detailing Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts. It focuses on how political extremist accounts on Facebook managed to bypass Meta's content reduction filters and grow in visibility, surpassing elected officials during recent elections.
description: An article covering Professor Fabio Giglietto's research analyzing over 2.5 million Facebook posts, focusing on how political extremist pages bypassed Meta's filters to gain visibility during elections.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE
description: News article from Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: This news article reports on the research led by Professor Fabio Giglietto regarding Meta's algorithm changes. It explains how institutional political figures in Italy suffered a massive drop in visibility on Facebook, prompting Italian politicians to discuss a bipartisan law to regulate digital platforms.
description: A news article detailing a study led by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino about how Meta's Facebook algorithm changes impact political visibility, prompting discussions for digital platform regulation in Italy.
